meta_commentary:
This is a 14-step plan across 5 phases touching frontend contexts, gallery interactions (click/lasso/drag), backend payload extension, and agent chat wiring. Key execution risks: (1) The GallerySelectionProvider must mount in AppProviders.tsx high enough for both GenerationsPane (layout-level) and AgentChat (VideoEditorShell-level) to consume it. (2) The normalizeSelectedClips function in the edge function currently requires clip_id to be non-empty — gallery items won't have meaningful clip_ids, so the validator must be relaxed to accept items with generation_id instead. (3) MediaGalleryItem is a shared component used across the app — changes to props/behavior must not break non-editor gallery views. (4) The lasso hook needs careful mousedown target detection to avoid conflicting with item drag-to-timeline. (5) Multi-item drag requires a new drag type that the timeline drop handler must explicitly check before the existing single-item path.

Execution order: T1-T3 are foundation (no UI changes, safe). T4-T6 add click/lasso/visual feedback. T7-T9 handle multi-drag. T10-T12 are backend (can parallel with T7-T9). T13-T14 wire everything together. T15 validates.

The SelectedMediaClip type currently has { clipId, assetKey, url, mediaType }. Gallery items won't have clipId or assetKey — the executor should use empty strings or generate synthetic IDs. The generationId field addition is the key discriminator.

For the normalizeSelectedClips edge function: it currently filters out items where clip_id is empty. The executor must update this to accept items where generation_id is present even if clip_id is empty, or use a synthetic clip_id like `gallery-{generationId}`.

tasks.description: Export buildSummary and ensure SelectedMediaClip type is reusable from useSelectedMediaClips.ts. Add optional generationId field to SelectedMediaClip. File: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useSelectedMediaClips.ts

Create GallerySelectionContext and provider in src/shared/contexts/GallerySelectionContext.tsx. State: selectedGalleryIds (ReadonlySet<string>), gallerySelectionMap (Map with url/mediaType/generationId). Actions: selectGalleryItem(id, meta, opts?:{toggle}), selectGalleryItems(items[]) for batch/lasso, clearGallerySelection(). Derived: selectedGalleryClips (SelectedMediaClip[] with generationId set), gallerySummary (via buildSummary). Filter: only allow items where type includes 'image' or 'video'. Import SelectedMediaClip and buildSummary from T1.
Mount GallerySelectionProvider in AppProviders.tsx composeProviders array, after PanesProvider (line 45). File: src/app/providers/AppProviders.tsx

Wire single-click and shift+click selection into GenerationsPane. In GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx: pass onImageClick handler to MediaGallery that calls selectGalleryItem from context. Create a small useModifierKeys() hook (~20 lines, tracks shiftKey via keydown/keyup) since onImageClick doesn't receive the native event. Single click replaces selection; shift+click toggles. Pass selectedIds from context to MediaGallery as new prop. Verify double-click/expand button still opens lightbox. Files: src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/components/GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx, new hook file for useModifierKeys.

Add lasso/marquee selection. Create src/features/gallery/components/GenerationsPane/hooks/useLassoSelection.ts. Track mousedown→mousemove→mouseup to draw a selection rectangle overlay. On mouseup, use getBoundingClientRect() on gallery items to find intersections, call selectGalleryItems(). Render semi-transparent rectangle (absolute div, border-sky-400 bg-sky-400/10). Guard: only initiate if mousedown target is gallery background/gap, not an item. Shift+lasso adds to existing selection. Attach to gallery container in GenerationsPaneGallery.tsx.

Add visual selection highlighting to MediaGalleryItem and MediaGallery. Add isSelected?:boolean prop to MediaGalleryItem — apply ring-2 ring-sky-400 rounded-lg when true. Add selectedIds?:ReadonlySet<string> prop to MediaGallery types.ts and thread through MediaGalleryGrid to each item computing isSelected={selectedIds?.has(image.id)}. Files: src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx, src/shared/components/MediaGallery/types.ts, MediaGalleryGrid, MediaGallery.tsx.

Extend drag data model in src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts. Add GENERATION_MULTI_DRAG_TYPE alongside existing types. Create setMultiGenerationDragData(event, items: GenerationDropData[]) that serializes array into dataTransfer. Create getMultiGenerationDropData(event): GenerationDropData[]|null. Update getDragType() to detect multi-generation drags.

Update MediaGalleryItem drag initiation. In handleDragStart (~line 261): check if dragged item is in gallery selection set and multiple items selected. If so, use setMultiGenerationDragData with all selected items' data. Otherwise fall back to single-item drag. Add count badge on drag preview for multi-item drags. Pass selectedItems and isSelected as props (MediaGalleryItem is shared, use props not context). File: src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx

Handle multi-item drops on timeline in useExternalDrop.ts. In onTimelineDrop (around line 346): check for multi-generation drop data first via getMultiGenerationDropData(event). If multi-drop: loop through each GenerationDropData, call registerGenerationAsset() for each, then handleAssetDrop() with staggered time offsets (5s for images, actual duration for videos, placed sequentially). Existing single-item path unchanged. File: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useExternalDrop.ts
Extend SelectedClipPayload in supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/types.ts: add optional generation_id?: string field (line 79-83).

Update normalizeSelectedClips in supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/index.ts (lines 11-31): extract generation_id from payload. IMPORTANT: currently the function requires clip_id to be non-empty — relax this so items with generation_id are accepted even if clip_id is empty (use synthetic ID like 'gallery-{generation_id}'). Update resolveClipGenerationIds in supabase/functions/ai-timeline-agent/tools/clips.ts (lines 4-15): after timeline-clip lookup fails, check if clip has generation_id field and use it directly as fallback.

Update frontend attachment pipeline in useAgentSession.ts (lines 270-276): when constructing selected_clips, include generation_id if present on the attachment. Add generationId to the AgentTurn attachment type in agent-session.ts (lines 12-16). Files: src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useAgentSession.ts, src/tools/video-editor/types/agent-session.ts

Merge gallery + timeline selections in AgentChat.tsx. Import useGallerySelection alongside useSelectedMediaClips (line 95). Concatenate clips from both sources, deduplicate by URL. Build combined summary via buildSummary. Update attachments construction (line 208-212) and summary display (line 384-387) to use merged list. After successful sendMessage.mutateAsync(), call clearGallerySelection(). File: src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChat.tsx

Update AgentChatMessage to display gallery attachment info if generationId is present. Check src/tools/video-editor/components/AgentChat/AgentChatMessage.tsx for any attachment rendering that needs the generationId field.

Run existing tests and verify changes. Find and run tests for: useSelectedMediaClips, useItemInteraction, useMediaGalleryViewInteractions, dragDrop utilities, and any agent-session related tests. Run TypeScript type checking (tsc --noEmit). Fix any failures. Write a small throwaway verification script that imports the new GallerySelectionContext and exercises selectGalleryItem/selectGalleryItems/clearGallerySelection to confirm the state management works, then delete it.

watch_items: PROVIDER-TREE: GallerySelectionProvider must be in AppProviders.tsx (not VideoEditorShell) because GenerationsPane is layout-level while AgentChat is inside VideoEditorShell. Verify both can consume the context.

NORMALIZE-VALIDATOR: normalizeSelectedClips in index.ts currently requires non-empty clip_id — gallery items have generation_id but no meaningful clip_id. The validator must be relaxed or gallery items will be silently dropped.

SHARED-COMPONENT: MediaGalleryItem.tsx is used across the entire app (not just editor gallery). New props (isSelected, selectedItems) must be optional and the component must render identically when they're absent.
LASSO-VS-DRAG: Lasso mousedown must only trigger on gallery background/gap, not on items. If it triggers on items, it will conflict with drag-to-timeline. Check event.target carefully.
MULTI-DRAG-ORDER: getMultiGenerationDropData must be checked BEFORE getGenerationDropData in useExternalDrop, otherwise multi-drags will be parsed as single-item drags (first item only).
DEDUP-BY-URL: When merging timeline + gallery clips in AgentChat, deduplicate by URL to avoid sending the same image twice if it appears in both selections.

BACKWARD-COMPAT: Timeline clips must continue working unchanged — they won't have generationId field. The backend fallback chain is: config.clips lookup → generation_id field → skip. Verify the existing path still works.
DEBT-WATCH: agent-chat-rendering debt item — ensure gallery attachments render correctly in both user and assistant turns. Don't introduce new rendering assumptions.
DEBT-WATCH: agent-chat-undo-integration — gallery selections clear after send, but the undo stack won't capture them. This is acceptable (same as timeline selection behavior).
LIGHTBOX-PRESERVATION: With enableSingleClick:true, verify the expand/eye button and double-click path to lightbox still work. The plan assumes these are independent of single-click.
EDGE-FUNCTION-DEPLOY: After T10-T11 changes, the edge function must be deployed before the frontend changes go live: npx supabase functions deploy ai-timeline-agent --project-ref wczysqzxlwdndgxitrvc

sense_checks.question: Does the generationId field on SelectedMediaClip have a sensible default/absence for timeline clips (which don't have it)?
Does the context filter out non-image/non-video items (effects, music) before adding to selectedGalleryIds?
Is the provider positioned after PanesProvider but accessible to both GenerationsPane (layout-level) and AgentChat (VideoEditorShell-level)?
Does single-click replace selection (not toggle), and does shift+click toggle? Is the useModifierKeys hook cleaning up event listeners on unmount?
Does lasso only initiate from background/gap clicks, not from item clicks? Does shift+lasso add to existing selection?
Does the selection ring (ring-2 ring-sky-400) match the timeline clip selection style? Does omitting selectedIds prop render items identically to before?
Is the new multi-drag MIME type distinct enough that getDragType() can reliably distinguish single vs multi generation drags?
When only one item is selected (or dragged item is not in selection), does drag behavior fall back to existing single-item behavior exactly?
Is multi-generation drop checked before single-generation drop in the handler? Are clips placed sequentially (not overlapping)?
Is generation_id optional and does adding it break no existing type consumers?
Does normalizeSelectedClips now accept items with generation_id but empty/missing clip_id? Does resolveClipGenerationIds fall back to generation_id when clip lookup fails?
Does the selected_clips payload include generation_id only when present (not sending undefined/null)?
Are gallery selections cleared after successful message send? Is deduplication working (same URL from timeline + gallery not sent twice)?
Do gallery attachments render properly in the chat message history with the generationId field present?
Do all existing tests pass (useSelectedMediaClips, useItemInteraction, useMediaGalleryViewInteractions)? Does tsc --noEmit pass with no type errors?

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Export buildSummary and SelectedMediaClip from useSelectedMediaClips
Step 2: Create GallerySelectionContext with selection state, actions, derived clips/summary
Step 3: Mount GallerySelectionProvider in AppProviders composeProviders array
Step 4: Wire single-click and shift+click selection in GenerationsPane
Step 5: Add lasso/marquee selection with useLassoSelection hook
Step 6: Visual selection highlighting on MediaGalleryItem and MediaGallery
Step 7: Extend drag data model with multi-generation drag type
Step 8: Update MediaGalleryItem drag initiation for multi-item drag
Step 9: Handle multi-item drops on timeline in useExternalDrop
Step 10: Extend SelectedClipPayload type with generation_id
Step 11: Update normalizeSelectedClips and resolveClipGenerationIds for gallery items
Step 12: Update frontend attachment pipeline in useAgentSession
Step 13: Merge gallery + timeline selections in AgentChat
Step 14: Update AgentTurn attachment type with generationId

validation.completeness_notes: T15 is the standard test/validation task not mapped to a specific plan step but required by execution protocol. All 14 plan steps are covered. Step 14 maps to both T12 (type change in agent-session.ts) and T14 (rendering in AgentChatMessage). T4 and T5 are independent (different interaction modes) and can be developed in parallel. T7-T9 (drag) and T10-T12 (backend) can be developed in parallel as the plan specifies.
